introSection:
Skytrak was a steel flying roller coaster located at Granada Studios in Manchester, England, UK.

The ride was the first flying coaster, in which passengers lie on their stomachs and look towards the ground.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The coaster was originally planned to open on 25 June 1997, but the opening was delayed due to maintenance issues, eventually opening in October. At that time, it was briefly operating. The ride was originally known as Gladiators Skytrak for a brief time, after the Granada TV show Gladiators.

In 1998, Granada Studios closed and Skytrak was scrapped.

fullText:
Skytrak was a steel flying roller coaster located at Granada Studios in Manchester, England, UK.

The ride was the first flying coaster, in which passengers lie on their stomachs and look towards the ground.

The coaster was originally planned to open on 25 June 1997, but the opening was delayed due to maintenance issues, eventually opening in October. At that time, it was briefly operating. The ride was originally known as Gladiators Skytrak for a brief time, after the Granada TV show Gladiators.

In 1998, Granada Studios closed and Skytrak was scrapped.
